papers.title: Glycaemic control and pregnancy outcomes with real-time continuous glucose monitoring in gestational diabetes (GRACE): an open-label, multicentre, multinational, randomised controlled trial
papers.title_ja: 妊娠糖尿病女性においてリアルタイム持続血糖モニタリングが巨大児出生を有意に減少させることを示したオープンラベル、多施設共同、ランダム化比較試験
papers.authors: Linder T, Dressler-Steinbach I, et al.
papers.abstract_en:
Background Data regarding the impact of real-time continuous glucose monitoring (rt-CGM) on reducing adverse pregnancy outcomes in women with gestational diabetes are contradictory. We aimed to assess differences in the proportion of large-for-gestational-age (LGA) newborns between women using rt-CGM versus self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG).
Methods For this open-label, parallel-group, multicentre, randomised controlled trial, women aged 18-55 years with singleton pregnancy and gestational diabetes (diagnosed according to the International Association of the Diabetes and Pregnancy Study Groups criteria), were randomly assigned (1:1) to rt-CGM or SMBG. The first allocation was by chance; for subsequent allocations, minimisation was used to balance three prespecified factors: gestational age at study entry, previous gestational diabetes, and preconceptional BMI. SMBG participants used blinded CGM for 10 days after randomisation and at 36–38 weeks; rt-CGM participants used open rt-CGM until delivery. All were managed according to standard care protocols in four university hospitals in Austria, Germany, and Switzerland. The primary endpoint was the proportion of LGA newborns (using the Perinatal Institute's GROW customised birthweight percentiles), assessed in the intention-to-treat population. Secondary endpoints included the requirement for glucose-lowering medication, CGM metrics, and non-glycaemic maternal and neonatal outcomes. Recruitment and follow-up are complete. This study is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT03981328).
Findings Between Aug 24, 2020, and May 30, 2024, 610 women were screened for eligibility, of whom 375 (diagnosed with gestational diabetes at a mean of 25.2 weeks [SD 2.3] of gestation), were randomly assigned to rt-CGM (n=190) or SMBG (n=185) at a mean of 28.6 weeks (SD 1.9) of gestation. 170 intervention and 175 control participants with available data were assessed for the primary endpoint. LGA neonates were born to six (4%) of 170 rt-CGM and 18 (10%) of 175 SMBG participants (OR 0.32, 95% CI 0.10-0.87, p=0.014). Small-for-gestational-age (SGA) neonates were born to 33 (19%) and 23 (13%) participants, respectively (OR 1.59, 0.86-2.99, p=0.11). Serious adverse events occurred in 23 (12%) of 190 versus 28 (15%) of 185 participants (OR 0.77, 0.42-1.40, p=0.39).
Interpretation rt-CGM use in women with gestational diabetes reduced LGA births, without differences in serious adverse events. The higher-than-expected overall prevalence of SGA infants, possibly related to the tight glycaemic control in our cohort, requires further research.

abstract_sections.label: 背景
abstract_sections.content: 妊娠糖尿病女性におけるリアルタイム持続血糖モニタリング（rt-CGM）が有害な妊娠アウトカムを減少させる効果に関するデータは矛盾している。本研究では、rt-CGM使用女性と自己血糖測定（SMBG）使用女性の間で巨大児（LGA）出生の割合に差があるかを評価することを目的とした。
abstract_sections.content_en: Data regarding the impact of real-time continuous glucose monitoring (rt-CGM) on reducing adverse pregnancy outcomes in women with gestational diabetes are contradictory. We aimed to assess differences in the proportion of large-for-gestational-age (LGA) newborns between women using rt-CGM versus self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG).

abstract_sections.label: 方法
abstract_sections.content: このオープンラベル、並行群間、多施設共同、ランダム化比較試験では、単胎妊娠で妊娠糖尿病と診断された（国際糖尿病・妊娠研究グループ基準による）18～55歳の女性をrt-CGM群またはSMBG群に1：1の比率でランダムに割り付けた。最初の割り付けは無作為であり、その後の割り付けには、研究開始時の妊娠週数、以前の妊娠糖尿病歴、妊娠前のBMIという3つの事前指定因子をバランスさせるために最小化法が用いられた。SMBG参加者はランダム化後および妊娠36～38週に盲検CGMを10日間使用し、rt-CGM参加者は出産までオープンrt-CGMを使用した。全参加者は、オーストリア、ドイツ、スイスの4つの大学病院で標準的なケアプロトコルに従って管理された。主要評価項目は、Perinatal InstituteのGROWカスタマイズ出生時体重パーセンタイルを用いたLGA新生児の割合で、intention-to-treat集団で評価された。副次評価項目には、血糖降下薬の必要性、CGM指標、非血糖関連の母体および新生児アウトカムが含まれた。募集と追跡調査は完了している。本研究はClinicalTrials.gov（NCT03981328）に登録されている。
abstract_sections.content_en: For this open-label, parallel-group, multicentre, randomised controlled trial, women aged 18-55 years with singleton pregnancy and gestational diabetes (diagnosed according to the International Association of the Diabetes and Pregnancy Study Groups criteria), were randomly assigned (1:1) to rt-CGM or SMBG. The first allocation was by chance; for subsequent allocations, minimisation was used to balance three prespecified factors: gestational age at study entry, previous gestational diabetes, and preconceptional BMI. SMBG participants used blinded CGM for 10 days after randomisation and at 36–38 weeks; rt-CGM participants used open rt-CGM until delivery. All were managed according to standard care protocols in four university hospitals in Austria, Germany, and Switzerland. The primary endpoint was the proportion of LGA newborns (using the Perinatal Institute's GROW customised birthweight percentiles), assessed in the intention-to-treat population. Secondary endpoints included the requirement for glucose-lowering medication, CGM metrics, and non-glycaemic maternal and neonatal outcomes. Recruitment and follow-up are complete. This study is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT03981328).

abstract_sections.label: 結果
abstract_sections.content: 2020年8月24日から2024年5月30日までに、適格性審査のため610人の女性がスクリーニングされ、そのうち375人（平均妊娠25.2週[SD 2.3]で妊娠糖尿病と診断）が平均妊娠28.6週[SD 1.9]でrt-CGM群（n=190）またはSMBG群（n=185）にランダムに割り付けられた。データが得られた介入群170人および対照群175人の参加者が主要評価項目の評価対象となった。LGA新生児は、rt-CGM群170人のうち6人（4%）とSMBG群175人のうち18人（10%）に生まれた（OR 0.32、95% CI 0.10-0.87、p=0.014）。在胎不当過小児（SGA）新生児は、それぞれ33人（19%）と23人（13%）に生まれた（OR 1.59、0.86-2.99、p=0.11）。重篤な有害事象は、rt-CGM群190人のうち23人（12%）とSMBG群185人のうち28人（15%）に発生した（OR 0.77、0.42-1.40、p=0.39）。
abstract_sections.content_en: Between Aug 24, 2020, and May 30, 2024, 610 women were screened for eligibility, of whom 375 (diagnosed with gestational diabetes at a mean of 25.2 weeks [SD 2.3] of gestation), were randomly assigned to rt-CGM (n=190) or SMBG (n=185) at a mean of 28.6 weeks (SD 1.9) of gestation. 170 intervention and 175 control participants with available data were assessed for the primary endpoint. LGA neonates were born to six (4%) of 170 rt-CGM and 18 (10%) of 175 SMBG participants (OR 0.32, 95% CI 0.10-0.87, p=0.014). Small-for-gestational-age (SGA) neonates were born to 33 (19%) and 23 (13%) participants, respectively (OR 1.59, 0.86-2.99, p=0.11). Serious adverse events occurred in 23 (12%) of 190 versus 28 (15%) of 185 participants (OR 0.77, 0.42-1.40, p=0.39).

abstract_sections.label: 結論
abstract_sections.content: 妊娠糖尿病女性におけるrt-CGMの使用は、重篤な有害事象に差がないままLGA出生を減少させた。SGA乳児の全体的な有病率が予想よりも高かったことは、本コホートにおける厳格な血糖コントロールに関連している可能性があり、さらなる研究が必要となる。
abstract_sections.content_en: rt-CGM use in women with gestational diabetes reduced LGA births, without differences in serious adverse events. The higher-than-expected overall prevalence of SGA infants, possibly related to the tight glycaemic control in our cohort, requires further research.

pico.P: 単胎妊娠で妊娠糖尿病と診断された18～55歳の女性
pico.I: リアルタイム持続血糖モニタリング (rt-CGM)
pico.O: 主要評価項目：巨大児（LGA）新生児の割合。rt-CGM群6人（4%） vs SMBG群18人（10%）（OR 0.32、95% CI 0.10-0.87、p=0.014）。

papers.title: Glycaemic control and pregnancy outcomes with real-time continuous glucose monitoring in gestational diabetes (GRACE): an open-label, multicentre, multinational, randomised controlled trial
papers.title_ja: 妊娠糖尿病女性におけるリアルタイム連続血糖モニタリング（rt-CGM）による血糖管理と妊娠アウトカム：多施設共同無作為化比較対照試験（GRACE）
papers.authors: Tina Linder, Iris Dressler-Steinbach, Silke Wegener, Karen Schellong, Saskia Schmidt, Daniel Eppel, Cécile Monod, Florian Heinzl, Katharina Redling, Bettina Winzeler, Beatrice Mosimann, Friederike Weschenfelder, Tanja Groten, Martina Mittlböck, Johan Jendle, Wolfgang Henrich, Micaela Morettini, Latife Bozkurt, Andrea Tura, Christian Göbl, the GRACE study collaborative group
papers.abstract_en:
Background Data regarding the impact of real-time continuous glucose monitoring (rt-CGM) on reducing adverse pregnancy outcomes in women with gestational diabetes are contradictory. We aimed to assess differences in the proportion of large-for-gestational-age (LGA) newborns between women using rt-CGM versus self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG).
Methods For this open-label, parallel-group, multicentre, randomised controlled trial, women aged 18-55 years with singleton pregnancy and gestational diabetes (diagnosed according to the International Association of the Diabetes and Pregnancy Study Groups criteria), were randomly assigned (1:1) to rt-CGM or SMBG. The first allocation was by chance; for subsequent allocations, minimisation was used to balance three prespecified factors: gestational age at study entry, previous gestational diabetes, and preconceptional BMI. SMBG participants used blinded CGM for 10 days after randomisation and at 36–38 weeks; rt-CGM participants used open rt-CGM until delivery. All were managed according to standard care protocols in four university hospitals in Austria, Germany, and Switzerland. The primary endpoint was the proportion of LGA newborns (using the Perinatal Institute's GROW customised birthweight percentiles), assessed in the intention-to-treat population. Secondary endpoints included the requirement for glucose-lowering medication, CGM metrics, and non-glycaemic maternal and neonatal outcomes. Recruitment and follow-up are complete. This study is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT03981328).
Findings Between Aug 24, 2020, and May 30, 2024, 610 women were screened for eligibility, of whom 375 (diagnosed with gestational diabetes at a mean of 25.2 weeks [SD 2.3] of gestation), were randomly assigned to rt-CGM (n=190) or SMBG (n=185) at a mean of 28.6 weeks (SD 1.9) of gestation. 170 intervention and 175 control participants with available data were assessed for the primary endpoint. LGA neonates were born to six (4%) of 170 rt-CGM and 18 (10%) of 175 SMBG participants (OR 0-32, 95% CI 0.10-0.87, p=0.014). Small-for-gestational-age (SGA) neonates were born to 33 (19%) and 23 (13%) participants, respectively (OR 1.59, 0-86-2-99, p=0.11). Serious adverse events occurred in 23 (12%) of 190 versus 28 (15%) of 185 participants (OR 0.77, 0-42-1-40, p=0.39).
Interpretation rt-CGM use in women with gestational diabetes reduced LGA births, without differences in serious adverse events. The higher-than-expected overall prevalence of SGA infants, possibly related to the tight glycaemic control in our cohort, requires further research.

abstract_sections.label: 背景
abstract_sections.content: 妊娠糖尿病女性におけるリアルタイム連続血糖モニタリング（rt-CGM）が妊娠中の有害転帰を減少させる効果に関するデータは矛盾している。本研究では、rt-CGMを使用する女性と血糖自己測定（SMBG）を行う女性の間で、large-for-gestational-age（LGA）新生児の割合に違いがあるかを評価することを目的とした。
abstract_sections.content_en: Data regarding the impact of real-time continuous glucose monitoring (rt-CGM) on reducing adverse pregnancy outcomes in women with gestational diabetes are contradictory. We aimed to assess differences in the proportion of large-for-gestational-age (LGA) newborns between women using rt-CGM versus self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG).

abstract_sections.label: 方法
abstract_sections.content: このオープンラベル、並行群間、多施設、多国籍、無作為化比較対照試験では、単胎妊娠および妊娠糖尿病（国際糖尿病妊娠研究会基準に従って診断）の18〜55歳の女性を対象とし、rt-CGMまたはSMBGに1:1で無作為に割り付けた。最初の割り付けは偶然によって行われ、その後の割り付けには、試験登録時の妊娠週数、以前の妊娠糖尿病の既往、妊娠前のBMIという3つの事前指定された要因のバランスを取るために最小化法が用いられた。SMBG参加者は、無作為化後および妊娠36〜38週目に10日間ブラインドCGMを使用した。rt-CGM参加者は出産までオープンrt-CGMを使用した。すべての参加者は、オーストリア、ドイツ、スイスの4つの大学病院で標準治療プロトコルに従って管理された。主要評価項目は、intention-to-treat集団で評価されたLGA新生児の割合（Perinatal InstituteのGROW個別化出生時体重パーセンタイルを使用）。副次評価項目には、血糖降下薬の必要性、CGM指標、非血糖性母体および新生児転帰が含まれた。登録と追跡調査は完了した。本研究はClinicalTrials.gov（NCT03981328）に登録されている。
abstract_sections.content_en: For this open-label, parallel-group, multicentre, randomised controlled trial, women aged 18-55 years with singleton pregnancy and gestational diabetes (diagnosed according to the International Association of the Diabetes and Pregnancy Study Groups criteria), were randomly assigned (1:1) to rt-CGM or SMBG. The first allocation was by chance; for subsequent allocations, minimisation was used to balance three prespecified factors: gestational age at study entry, previous gestational diabetes, and preconceptional BMI. SMBG participants used blinded CGM for 10 days after randomisation and at 36–38 weeks; rt-CGM participants used open rt-CGM until delivery. All were managed according to standard care protocols in four university hospitals in Austria, Germany, and Switzerland. The primary endpoint was the proportion of LGA newborns (using the Perinatal Institute's GROW customised birthweight percentiles), assessed in the intention-to-treat population. Secondary endpoints included the requirement for glucose-lowering medication, CGM metrics, and non-glycaemic maternal and neonatal outcomes. Recruitment and follow-up are complete. This study is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT03981328).

abstract_sections.label: 結果
abstract_sections.content: 2020年8月24日から2024年5月30日の間に、610人の女性が適格性についてスクリーニングされ、そのうち375人（妊娠25.2週[SD 2.3]で妊娠糖尿病と診断）が、妊娠28.6週[SD 1.9]でrt-CGM（n=190）またはSMBG（n=185）に無作為に割り付けられた。利用可能なデータを持つ170人の介入群参加者と175人の対照群参加者が主要評価項目について評価された。LGA新生児は、rt-CGM群170人中6人（4%）とSMBG群175人中18人（10%）に生まれた（OR 0.32, 95% CI 0.10-0.87, p=0.014）。small-for-gestational-age（SGA）新生児は、それぞれ33人（19%）と23人（13%）に生まれた（OR 1.59, 0.86-2.99, p=0.11）。重篤な有害事象は、rt-CGM群190人中23人（12%）対SMBG群185人中28人（15%）に発生した（OR 0.77, 0.42-1.40, p=0.39）。
abstract_sections.content_en: Between Aug 24, 2020, and May 30, 2024, 610 women were screened for eligibility, of whom 375 (diagnosed with gestational diabetes at a mean of 25.2 weeks [SD 2.3] of gestation), were randomly assigned to rt-CGM (n=190) or SMBG (n=185) at a mean of 28.6 weeks (SD 1.9) of gestation. 170 intervention and 175 control participants with available data were assessed for the primary endpoint. LGA neonates were born to six (4%) of 170 rt-CGM and 18 (10%) of 175 SMBG participants (OR 0-32, 95% CI 0.10-0.87, p=0.014). Small-for-gestational-age (SGA) neonates were born to 33 (19%) and 23 (13%) participants, respectively (OR 1.59, 0-86-2-99, p=0.11). Serious adverse events occurred in 23 (12%) of 190 versus 28 (15%) of 185 participants (OR 0.77, 0-42-1-40, p=0.39).

abstract_sections.label: 結論
abstract_sections.content: 妊娠糖尿病女性におけるrt-CGMの使用は、重篤な有害事象に差がない状態でLGA出生を減少させた。SGA乳児の全体的な有病率が予想よりも高かったことは、本コホートにおける厳格な血糖管理に関連している可能性があり、さらなる研究が必要である。
abstract_sections.content_en: rt-CGM use in women with gestational diabetes reduced LGA births, without differences in serious adverse events. The higher-than-expected overall prevalence of SGA infants, possibly related to the tight glycaemic control in our cohort, requires further research.

pico.P: 妊娠糖尿病の女性（18-55歳、単胎妊娠）
pico.I: リアルタイム連続血糖モニタリング (rt-CGM)
pico.O:
主要アウトカムはLGA新生児の割合。rt-CGM群6/170 (4%)、SMBG群18/175 (10%) (OR 0.32, 95% CI 0.10-0.87, p=0.014)。rt-CGM群で有意に減少した。
副次アウトカムのSGA新生児の割合は、rt-CGM群33/170 (19%)、SMBG群23/175 (13%) (OR 1.59, 0.86-2.99, p=0.11)。有意差なし。
重篤な有害事象は、rt-CGM群23/190 (12%)、SMBG群28/185 (15%) (OR 0.77, 0.42-1.40, p=0.39)。有意差なし。

papers.title: Tirzepatide for Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction and Obesity
papers.title_ja: チルゼパチドは、肥満を伴う駆出率保持型心不全患者において、心血管死または心不全悪化イベントのリスクを低下させ、健康状態を改善する
papers.journal: The NEW ENGLAND JOURNAL of MEDICINE
papers.authors: Milton Packer, M.D., Michael R. Zile, M.D., Christopher M. Kramer, M.D., Seth J. Baum, M.D., Sheldon E. Litwin, M.D., Venu Menon, M.D., Junbo Ge, M.D., Govinda J. Weerakkody, Ph.D., Yang Ou, Ph.D., Mathijs C. Bunck, M.D., Karla C. Hurt, B.S.N., Masahiro Murakami, M.D., and Barry A. Borlaug, M.D., for the SUMMIT Trial Study Group*
papers.abstract_en:
BACKGROUND
Obesity increases the risk of heart failure with preserved ejection fraction. Tirzepatide, a long-acting agonist of glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide and glucagon-like peptide-1 receptors, causes considerable weight loss, but data are lacking with respect to its effects on cardiovascular outcomes.
METHODS
In this international, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial, we randomly assigned, in a 1:1 ratio, 731 patients with heart failure, an ejection fraction of at least 50%, and a body-mass index (the weight in kilograms divided by the square of the height in meters) of at least 30 to receive tirzepatide (up to 15 mg subcutaneously once per week) or placebo for at least 52 weeks. The two primary end points were a composite of adjudicated death from cardiovascular causes or a worsening heart-failure event (assessed in a time-to-first-event analysis) and the change from baseline to 52 weeks in the Kansas City Cardiomyopathy Questionnaire clinical summary score (KCCQ-CSS; scores range from 0 to 100, with higher scores indicating better quality of life).
RESULTS
A total of 364 patients were assigned to the tirzepatide group and 367 to the placebo group; the median duration of follow-up was 104 weeks. Adjudicated death from cardiovascular causes or a worsening heart-failure event occurred in 36 patients (9.9%) in the tirzepatide group and in 56 patients (15.3%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.62; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.41 to 0.95; P=0.026). Worsening heart-failure events occurred in 29 patients (8.0%) in the tirzepatide group and in 52 patients (14.2%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.54; 95% CI, 0.34 to 0.85), and adjudicated death from cardiovascular causes occurred in 8 patients (2.2%) and 5 patients (1.4%), respectively (hazard ratio, 1.58; 95% CI, 0.52 to 4.83). At 52 weeks, the mean (±SD) change in the KCCQ-CSS was 19.5±1.2 in the tirzepatide group as compared with 12.7±1.3 in the placebo group (between-group difference, 6.9; 95% CI, 3.3 to 10.6; P<0.001). Adverse events (mainly gastrointestinal) leading to discontinuation of the trial drug occurred in 23 patients (6.3%) in the tirzepatide group and in 5 patients (1.4%) in the placebo group.
CONCLUSIONS
Treatment with tirzepatide led to a lower risk of a composite of death from cardiovascular causes or worsening heart failure than placebo and improved health status in patients with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction and obesity. (Funded by Eli Lilly; SUMMIT ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT04847557.)

abstract_sections.label: 背景
abstract_sections.content: 肥満は、駆出率保持型心不全のリスクを高めます。グルコース依存性インスリン分泌刺激ポリペプチドおよびグルカゴン様ペプチド-1受容体の長時間作用型アゴニストであるチルゼパチドは、かなりの体重減少を引き起こしますが、心血管アウトカムに対するその効果に関するデータは不足しています。
abstract_sections.content_en: Obesity increases the risk of heart failure with preserved ejection fraction. Tirzepatide, a long-acting agonist of glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide and glucagon-like peptide-1 receptors, causes considerable weight loss, but data are lacking with respect to its effects on cardiovascular outcomes.

abstract_sections.label: 方法
abstract_sections.content: この国際的な二重盲検無作為化プラセボ対照試験では、心不全、駆出率50%以上、およびボディマス指数（BMI）30以上の患者731人を、チルゼパチド（週1回最大15mg皮下投与）またはプラセボを少なくとも52週間受ける群に1:1の比率で無作為に割り付けました。2つの主要評価項目は、心血管原因による裁定済み死亡または心不全悪化イベントの複合（初回イベントまでの期間分析で評価）と、ベースラインから52週までのカンザスシティ心筋症質問票臨床サマリースコア（KCCQ-CSS；スコアは0から100の範囲で、高いスコアほど生活の質が高いことを示す）の変化でした。
abstract_sections.content_en: In this international, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial, we randomly assigned, in a 1:1 ratio, 731 patients with heart failure, an ejection fraction of at least 50%, and a body-mass index (the weight in kilograms divided by the square of the height in meters) of at least 30 to receive tirzepatide (up to 15 mg subcutaneously once per week) or placebo for at least 52 weeks. The two primary end points were a composite of adjudicated death from cardiovascular causes or a worsening heart-failure event (assessed in a time-to-first-event analysis) and the change from baseline to 52 weeks in the Kansas City Cardiomyopathy Questionnaire clinical summary score (KCCQ-CSS; scores range from 0 to 100, with higher scores indicating better quality of life).

abstract_sections.label: 結果
abstract_sections.content: 合計364人の患者がチルゼパチド群に、367人がプラセボ群に割り付けられました。追跡期間の中央値は104週でした。心血管原因による裁定済み死亡または心不全悪化イベントは、チルゼパチド群で36人（9.9%）、プラセボ群で56人（15.3%）に発生しました（ハザード比0.62；95%信頼区間[CI]、0.41〜0.95；P=0.026）。心不全悪化イベントは、チルゼパチド群で29人（8.0%）、プラセボ群で52人（14.2%）に発生しました（ハザード比0.54；95% CI、0.34〜0.85）。心血管原因による裁定済み死亡は、それぞれ8人（2.2%）と5人（1.4%）に発生しました（ハザード比1.58；95% CI、0.52〜4.83）。52週時点でのKCCQ-CSSの平均（±SD）変化は、チルゼパチド群で19.5±1.2、プラセボ群で12.7±1.3でした（群間差、6.9；95% CI、3.3〜10.6；P<0.001）。治験薬中止に至った有害事象（主に胃腸障害）は、チルゼパチド群で23人（6.3%）、プラセボ群で5人（1.4%）に発生しました。
abstract_sections.content_en: A total of 364 patients were assigned to the tirzepatide group and 367 to the placebo group; the median duration of follow-up was 104 weeks. Adjudicated death from cardiovascular causes or a worsening heart-failure event occurred in 36 patients (9.9%) in the tirzepatide group and in 56 patients (15.3%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.62; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.41 to 0.95; P=0.026). Worsening heart-failure events occurred in 29 patients (8.0%) in the tirzepatide group and in 52 patients (14.2%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.54; 95% CI, 0.34 to 0.85), and adjudicated death from cardiovascular causes occurred in 8 patients (2.2%) and 5 patients (1.4%), respectively (hazard ratio, 1.58; 95% CI, 0.52 to 4.83). At 52 weeks, the mean (±SD) change in the KCCQ-CSS was 19.5±1.2 in the tirzepatide group as compared with 12.7±1.3 in the placebo group (between-group difference, 6.9; 95% CI, 3.3 to 10.6; P<0.001). Adverse events (mainly gastrointestinal) leading to discontinuation of the trial drug occurred in 23 patients (6.3%) in the tirzepatide group and in 5 patients (1.4%) in the placebo group.

abstract_sections.label: 結論
abstract_sections.content: チルゼパチドによる治療は、肥満を伴う駆出率保持型心不全患者において、プラセボと比較して心血管原因による死亡または心不全悪化の複合リスクを低下させ、健康状態を改善しました。（Eli Lillyが出資；SUMMIT ClinicalTrials.gov番号、NCT04847557）。
abstract_sections.content_en: Treatment with tirzepatide led to a lower risk of a composite of death from cardiovascular causes or worsening heart failure than placebo and improved health status in patients with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction and obesity. (Funded by Eli Lilly; SUMMIT ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT04847557.)

pico.P: 心不全（NYHAクラスII～IV）、左室駆出率50%以上、BMI 30以上、6分間歩行距離100～425m、KCCQ-CSS 80以下の患者731人。
pico.I: チルゼパチド（最大15mg皮下投与、週1回）
pico.O:
主要評価項目1: 心血管原因による裁定済み死亡または心不全悪化イベントの複合（初回イベントまでの期間分析）。結果: チルゼパチド群 9.9% vs プラセボ群 15.3%。ハザード比 0.62；95% CI, 0.41～0.95；P=0.026。
主要評価項目2: 52週時点でのKCCQ-CSSのベースラインからの変化。結果: チルゼパチド群 19.5±1.2 vs プラセボ群 12.7±1.3。群間差 6.9；95% CI, 3.3～10.6；P<0.001。

papers.title: Discontinuation of Levothyroxine in Adults Aged 60 Years or Older
papers.title_ja: 60歳以上の成人におけるレボチロキシンの中止可能性と成功率
papers.authors: Ravensberg J, Gussekloo J, et al.
papers.abstract_en:
IMPORTANCE Many adults aged 60 years or older take the thyroid hormone levothyroxine, which is generally continued for life. However, it is uncertain whether long-term continuation is always necessary.
OBJECTIVE To determine the percentage of adults aged 60 years or older who can successfully discontinue levothyroxine treatment.
DESIGN, SETTING, AND PARTICIPANTS This single-group, prospective study included community-dwelling adults aged 60 years or older who were taking levothyroxine at a stable dosage (≤150 µg/d) for at least 1 year and had a thyrotropin (TSH) level of less than 10 mIU/L. The study was conducted at 58 general practices in the Netherlands. Participants were enrolled between January 2020 and July 2022 and final follow-up occurred on December 12, 2023.
INTERVENTIONS Open-label, protocol-driven, stepwise dose reduction with thyroid function testing performed at least 6 weeks after each reduction in levothyroxine dose.
MAIN OUTCOMES AND MEASURES The primary outcome was the proportion of participants who discontinued levothyroxine and had both a thyrotropin level of less than 10 mIU/L and a free thyroxine level within the reference range at 1 year after the start of discontinuation. The secondary outcomes included factors associated with successful discontinuation (demographics, thyroid function, dose) and thyroid-related quality of life.
RESULTS Of the 370 participants who started the levothyroxine discontinuation phase (median age, 70 [range, 60-89] years; 80% female; median thyrotropin level, 2.2 [range, 0.02-9.69] mIU/L; mean free thyroxine level, 1.21 [SD, 0.18] ng/dL), 366 completed final follow-up at 1 year. Of the 370 participants (median levothyroxine dose at baseline, 50 [range, 12.5-150] µg/d), 95 (25.7% [95% CI, 21.5%-30.4%]) successfully stopped taking levothyroxine and had a median thyrotropin level of 5.03 mIU/L (range, 1.56-9.40 mIU/L) and a mean free thyroxine level of 1.01 ng/dL (range, 0.80-1.43 ng/dL) at 1 year. Of the 95 participants who successfully discontinued levothyroxine, 46 (48.4% [95% CI, 38.6%-58.3%]) had a thyrotropin level of less than 4.8 mIU/L. Among the 88 participants taking a levothyroxine dose of 50 µg/d or lower, 56 (63.6%) successfully discontinued this treatment. Thyroid-related quality of life showed no clinically relevant changes overall from baseline to 1 year and stratified by successful levothyroxine discontinuation vs unsuccessful discontinuation.
CONCLUSIONS AND RELEVANCE In this open-label, single-group prospective study, 25.7% of adults aged 60 years or older discontinued levothyroxine treatment while maintaining adequate thyroid function at 1 year. Evaluation of the need to continue levothyroxine should be considered in adults aged 60 years or older, particularly in those taking a dose of 50 µg/d or lower.

abstract_sections.label: 背景
abstract_sections.content: 60歳以上の多くの成人は甲状腺ホルモン薬であるレボチロキシンを服用しており、通常は生涯にわたって継続されます。しかし、長期的な継続が常に必要であるかは不明です。
abstract_sections.content_en: Many adults aged 60 years or older take the thyroid hormone levothyroxine, which is generally continued for life. However, it is uncertain whether long-term continuation is always necessary.

abstract_sections.label: 目的
abstract_sections.content: 60歳以上の成人がレボチロキシン治療を正常に中止できる割合を決定すること。
abstract_sections.content_en: To determine the percentage of adults aged 60 years or older who can successfully discontinue levothyroxine treatment.

abstract_sections.label: デザイン、設定、参加者
abstract_sections.content: この単一群前向き研究には、安定した用量（150 µg/日以下）で少なくとも1年間レボチロキシンを服用しており、甲状腺刺激ホルモン（TSH）レベルが10 mIU/L未満であった60歳以上の地域在住成人が含まれました。研究はオランダの58の一般診療所で実施されました。参加者は2020年1月から2022年7月の間に登録され、最終フォローアップは2023年12月12日に行われました。
abstract_sections.content_en: This single-group, prospective study included community-dwelling adults aged 60 years or older who were taking levothyroxine at a stable dosage (≤150 µg/d) for at least 1 year and had a thyrotropin (TSH) level of less than 10 mIU/L. The study was conducted at 58 general practices in the Netherlands. Participants were enrolled between January 2020 and July 2022 and final follow-up occurred on December 12, 2023.

abstract_sections.label: 介入
abstract_sections.content: 公開標識、プロトコルに基づいた段階的な用量削減で、レボチロキシン用量削減ごとに少なくとも6週間後に甲状腺機能検査が実施されました。
abstract_sections.content_en: Open-label, protocol-driven, stepwise dose reduction with thyroid function testing performed at least 6 weeks after each reduction in levothyroxine dose.

abstract_sections.label: 主要アウトカムと測定
abstract_sections.content: 主要アウトカムは、レボチロキシンを中止し、中止開始から1年後に甲状腺刺激ホルモンレベルが10 mIU/L未満かつ遊離サイロキシンレベルが基準範囲内であった参加者の割合でした。副次アウトカムには、成功した中止に関連する因子（人口統計、甲状腺機能、用量）および甲状腺関連の生活の質が含まれました。
abstract_sections.content_en: The primary outcome was the proportion of participants who discontinued levothyroxine and had both a thyrotropin level of less than 10 mIU/L and a free thyroxine level within the reference range at 1 year after the start of discontinuation. The secondary outcomes included factors associated with successful discontinuation (demographics, thyroid function, dose) and thyroid-related quality of life.

abstract_sections.label: 結果
abstract_sections.content: レボチロキシン中止フェーズを開始した370人の参加者（中央年齢70歳[範囲60-89歳]、女性80%、中央TSHレベル2.2 mIU/L[範囲0.02-9.69 mIU/L]、平均遊離サイロキシンレベル1.21 ng/dL[SD 0.18 ng/dL]）のうち、366人が1年後の最終フォローアップを完了しました。370人の参加者（ベースライン時の中央レボチロキシン用量50 µg/日[範囲12.5-150 µg/日]）のうち、95人（25.7%[95% CI, 21.5%-30.4%]）がレボチロキシン服用を成功裏に中止し、1年後に中央TSHレベル5.03 mIU/L（範囲1.56-9.40 mIU/L）および平均遊離サイロキシンレベル1.01 ng/dL（範囲0.80-1.43 ng/dL）を維持していました。レボチロキシンの中止に成功した95人の参加者のうち、46人（48.4%[95% CI, 38.6%-58.3%]）がTSHレベル4.8 mIU/L未満でした。レボチロキシン50 µg/日以下の用量を服用していた88人の参加者のうち、56人（63.6%）がこの治療を成功裏に中止しました。甲状腺関連の生活の質は、ベースラインから1年後まで、全体として臨床的に関連のある変化を示さず、成功した中止群と不成功群で層別化しても同様でした。
abstract_sections.content_en: Of the 370 participants who started the levothyroxine discontinuation phase (median age, 70 [range, 60-89] years; 80% female; median thyrotropin level, 2.2 [range, 0.02-9.69] mIU/L; mean free thyroxine level, 1.21 [SD, 0.18] ng/dL), 366 completed final follow-up at 1 year. Of the 370 participants (median levothyroxine dose at baseline, 50 [range, 12.5-150] µg/d), 95 (25.7% [95% CI, 21.5%-30.4%]) successfully stopped taking levothyroxine and had a median thyrotropin level of 5.03 mIU/L (range, 1.56-9.40 mIU/L) and a mean free thyroxine level of 1.01 ng/dL (range, 0.80-1.43 ng/dL) at 1 year. Of the 95 participants who successfully discontinued levothyroxine, 46 (48.4% [95% CI, 38.6%-58.3%]) had a thyrotropin level of less than 4.8 mIU/L. Among the 88 participants taking a levothyroxine dose of 50 µg/d or lower, 56 (63.6%) successfully discontinued this treatment. Thyroid-related quality of life showed no clinically relevant changes overall from baseline to 1 year and stratified by successful levothyroxine discontinuation vs unsuccessful discontinuation.

abstract_sections.label: 結論と関連性
abstract_sections.content: この公開標識、単一群前向き研究では、60歳以上の成人25.7%がレボチロキシン治療を中止し、1年間にわたり適切な甲状腺機能を維持しました。レボチロキシン継続の必要性の評価は、60歳以上の成人、特に50 µg/日以下の用量を服用している成人で考慮されるべきです。
abstract_sections.content_en: In this open-label, single-group prospective study, 25.7% of adults aged 60 years or older discontinued levothyroxine treatment while maintaining adequate thyroid function at 1 year. Evaluation of the need to continue levothyroxine should be considered in adults aged 60 years or older, particularly in those taking a dose of 50 µg/d or lower.

pico.P: 60歳以上で、安定した用量（150 µg/日以下）のレボチロキシンを少なくとも1年間服用し、TSHレベルが10 mIU/L未満であった地域在住成人
pico.I: 公開標識、プロトコルに基づいた段階的なレボチロキシン用量削減プロトコル（用量削減ごとに少なくとも6週間後に甲状腺機能検査を実施）
pico.O: レボチロキシンを中止し、中止開始から1年後にTSHレベルが10 mIU/L未満かつ遊離サイロキシンレベルが基準範囲内であった参加者の割合。結果：95人 (25.7% [95% CI, 21.5%-30.4%]) が成功裏に中止。

papers.memo:
甲状腺ホルモン製剤であるレボチロキシンは、60歳以上の甲状腺機能低下症では一般に生涯にわたって継続投与されるが、長期の投与が常に必要かは定かでないという。オランダ・ライデン大学医療センターのJanneke Ravensberg氏らは、安定用量のレボチロキシンの投与を1年以上受けていた甲状腺機能低下症の60歳以上の集団では、その約4分の1が、投与中止から1年後も適切な甲状腺機能を維持し、甲状腺関連の生活の質（QOL）にも臨床的に意義のある変化を認めないことを示した。研究の成果は、JAMA誌オンライン版2026年4月6日号で報告された。

オランダのプライマリケアの単群前向きコホート研究
　研究グループは、レボチロキシンの投与を受けている甲状腺機能低下症の年齢60歳以上の集団における投与中止の成功率を明らかにする目的で、オランダの58の一般診療施設で非盲検単群前向きコホート研究を実施した（ZonMwの助成を受けた）。2020年1月～2022年7月に被験者の登録を行った。
　対象は、少なくとも1年間、安定用量（150μg/日以下）のレボチロキシンの投与を受け、甲状腺刺激ホルモン（TSH）値が10mIU/L未満の60歳以上の地域在住者とした。
　レボチロキシンの用量を段階的に減量し、各減量時から少なくとも6週間が経過した後に甲状腺機能検査を行った。
　主要アウトカムは、レボチロキシンの投与を中止して1年後に、投与中止を継続しており、かつTSH値が10mIU/L未満で、遊離サイロキシン値が基準値の範囲内にある被験者の割合であった。

投与量が少ないほど、中止の成功率が高い
　370例を登録した。ベースラインの年齢中央値は70.5歳（四分位範囲[IQR]：65.7～75.7、範囲：60.6～89.0）で、294例（79.5％）が女性であった。平均レボチロキシン投与量は84（SD 31）μg/日であり、TSH値中央値は2.2mIU/L（IQR：1.1～3.5、範囲：0.02～9.69）、平均遊離サイロキシン値は1.21（SD 0.18）ng/dLであった。このうち366例が1年後の最終追跡調査を完了した。
　370例中95例（25.7％、95％信頼区間[CI]：21.5～30.4）が、1年後の時点でレボチロキシンの投与中止を継続しており、その時点のTSH値中央値は5.03mIU/L（範囲：1.56～9.40）、平均遊離サイロキシン値は1.01ng/dL（範囲：0.80～1.43）であった。
　レボチロキシンの投与中止に成功した95例のうち、46例（48.4％、95％CI：38.6～58.3）の1年後のTSH値は4.8mIU/L未満であった。
　また、ベースラインのレボチロキシン投与量が少ないほど、投与中止の成功率が高かった。レボチロキシン50μg/日以下の投与を受けていた88例のうち、56例（63.6％）がこの治療の中止に成功し、75μg/日以下の投与を受けていた176例では、79例（44.9％）が中止の成功に至った。

介入に関連した重篤な有害事象はない
　甲状腺関連QOLは、ベースラインからその後1年の時点まで、全体として臨床的に意義のある変化を認めなかった。また、レボチロキシン中止の成功例と不成功例に層別化しても、QOLの差が臨床的に意義のある最小変化量（MCID）を超えることはなかった。

　平均12.9ヵ月の追跡期間中に、16例に17件の重篤な有害事象（予定外の入院15件、死亡2件）が発現したが、試験介入に関連したものはなかった。
　著者は、「60歳以上、とくにレボチロキシン50μg/日以下の投与を受けている患者において、レボチロキシン治療の継続の必要性について評価を行うべきである」としている。

papers.title: Three Low-Dose Antihypertensive Agents in a Single Pill after Intracerebral Hemorrhage
papers.title_ja: 脳内出血後の再発性脳卒中および主要心血管イベントを低減する低用量3剤合剤の有効性
papers.journal: The New England Journal of Medicine
papers.authors: Craig S. Anderson, Lili Song, et al.
papers.abstract_en:
BACKGROUND
Blood-pressure reduction is the only proven treatment to prevent stroke due to intra-cerebral hemorrhage. Whether a single pill that combines three antihypertensive drugs at low doses, in addition to standard antihypertensive treatment, can lower blood pressure more than standard care alone and reduce the risk of recurrent stroke after intracerebral hemorrhage is uncertain.
METHODS
We conducted a multinational, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial involving patients with a history of intracerebral hemorrhage. Patients were eligible for the trial if they had a systolic blood pressure of 130 to 160 mm Hg at baseline and were in clinically stable condition. After a 2-week active run-in phase during which all the patients received a once-daily pill containing three antihypertensive agents at low doses (telmisartan at 20 mg, amlodipine at 2.5 mg, and indapamide at 1.25 mg; the triple pill), the patients were randomly assigned to continue receiving the triple pill or to receive matching placebo. The primary outcome was the first recurrent stroke. Secondary outcomes included blood-pressure control, major cardio-vascular events, death from cardiovascular causes, and safety.
RESULTS
Of 1670 patients who underwent randomization, 833 were assigned to receive the triple pill and 837 to receive placebo. The mean age of the patients was 58 years. At a median follow-up of 2.5 years, recurrent stroke had occurred in 38 patients (4.6%) in the triple-pill group and 62 (7.4%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% confi-dence interval [CI], 0.41 to 0.92; P=0.02). The mean systolic blood pressure during follow-up was 127 mm Hg and 138 mm Hg, respectively. The incidence of major cardiovascular events was lower with the triple pill than with placebo (6.6% vs. 9.8%; P=0.04). Serious adverse events occurred in 23.2% of the patients in the triple-pill group and 26.0% of those in the placebo group. Early discontinuation of the trial regimen due to an adverse event occurred in 13.6% and 6.0%, respectively. The most common adverse event leading to discontinuation was an increase of 20% or more in the serum creatinine level.
CONCLUSIONS
Among patients with intracerebral hemorrhage, treatment with a combination of three low-dose antihypertensive agents in a single pill, in addition to standard care, was associated with a lower incidence of recurrent stroke and major cardiovascular events than placebo. (Funded by the National Health and Medical Research Coun-cil of Australia and the Brazilian Ministry of Health; TRIDENT ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT02699645; Australian New Zealand Clinical Trials Registry number, ACTRN12616000327482.)

abstract_sections.label: 背景
abstract_sections.content: 血圧低下は、脳内出血による脳卒中を予防する唯一の確立された治療法である。低用量の3種類の降圧薬を単一の錠剤に組み合わせたものが、標準的な降圧治療に加えて、標準治療単独よりも血圧をさらに低下させ、脳内出血後の再発性脳卒中のリスクを軽減できるかどうかは不明である。
abstract_sections.content_en: Blood-pressure reduction is the only proven treatment to prevent stroke due to intra-cerebral hemorrhage. Whether a single pill that combines three antihypertensive drugs at low doses, in addition to standard antihypertensive treatment, can lower blood pressure more than standard care alone and reduce the risk of recurrent stroke after intracerebral hemorrhage is uncertain.

abstract_sections.label: 方法
abstract_sections.content: 我々は、脳内出血の既往を持つ患者を対象とした多施設共同、二重盲検、無作為化、プラセボ対照試験を実施した。ベースライン時に収縮期血圧が130〜160 mmHgであり、臨床的に安定した状態の患者が試験の対象となった。2週間のアクティブ・ランイン期間中、全患者が低用量の3種類の降圧薬（テルミサルタン20mg、アムロジピン2.5mg、インダパミド1.25mg；トリプルピル）を含む1日1回の錠剤を服用した後、患者はトリプルピルを引き続き服用する群またはプラセボを服用する群に無作為に割り付けられた。主要評価項目は最初の再発性脳卒中であった。副次評価項目には、血圧コントロール、主要心血管イベント、心血管原因による死亡、および安全性が含まれた。
abstract_sections.content_en: We conducted a multinational, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial involving patients with a history of intracerebral hemorrhage. Patients were eligible for the trial if they had a systolic blood pressure of 130 to 160 mm Hg at baseline and were in clinically stable condition. After a 2-week active run-in phase during which all the patients received a once-daily pill containing three antihypertensive agents at low doses (telmisartan at 20 mg, amlodipine at 2.5 mg, and indapamide at 1.25 mg; the triple pill), the patients were randomly assigned to continue receiving the triple pill or to receive matching placebo. The primary outcome was the first recurrent stroke. Secondary outcomes included blood-pressure control, major cardio-vascular events, death from cardiovascular causes, and safety.

abstract_sections.label: 結果
abstract_sections.content: 無作為化された1670人の患者のうち、833人がトリプルピル群に、837人がプラセボ群に割り当てられた。患者の平均年齢は58歳であった。中央値2.5年の追跡期間で、再発性脳卒中はトリプルピル群で38人（4.6%）、プラセボ群で62人（7.4%）に発生した（ハザード比0.61；95%信頼区間[CI] 0.41〜0.92；P=0.02）。追跡期間中の平均収縮期血圧は、それぞれ127mmHgと138mmHgであった。主要心血管イベントの発生率は、トリプルピル群の方がプラセボ群よりも低かった（6.6% vs. 9.8%；P=0.04）。重篤な有害事象は、トリプルピル群の23.2%、プラセボ群の26.0%に発生した。有害事象による試験レジメンの早期中止は、それぞれ13.6%と6.0%に発生した。中止に至った最も一般的な有害事象は、血清クレアチニンレベルの20%以上の増加であった。
abstract_sections.content_en: Of 1670 patients who underwent randomization, 833 were assigned to receive the triple pill and 837 to receive placebo. The mean age of the patients was 58 years. At a median follow-up of 2.5 years, recurrent stroke had occurred in 38 patients (4.6%) in the triple-pill group and 62 (7.4%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% confi-dence interval [CI], 0.41 to 0.92; P=0.02). The mean systolic blood pressure during follow-up was 127 mm Hg and 138 mm Hg, respectively. The incidence of major cardiovascular events was lower with the triple pill than with placebo (6.6% vs. 9.8%; P=0.04). Serious adverse events occurred in 23.2% of the patients in the triple-pill group and 26.0% of those in the placebo group. Early discontinuation of the trial regimen due to an adverse event occurred in 13.6% and 6.0%, respectively. The most common adverse event leading to discontinuation was an increase of 20% or more in the serum creatinine level.

abstract_sections.label: 結論
abstract_sections.content: 脳内出血の患者において、標準治療に加えて、低用量の3種類の降圧薬を単一の錠剤で組み合わせた治療は、プラセボと比較して再発性脳卒中および主要心血管イベントの発生率低下と関連していた。（オーストラリア国立保健医療研究評議会およびブラジル保健省からの資金提供；TRIDENT ClinicalTrials.gov登録番号、NCT02699645；Australian New Zealand Clinical Trials Registry番号、ACTRN12616000327482。）
abstract_sections.content_en: Among patients with intracerebral hemorrhage, treatment with a combination of three low-dose antihypertensive agents in a single pill, in addition to standard care, was associated with a lower incidence of recurrent stroke and major cardiovascular events than placebo. (Funded by the National Health and Medical Research Coun-cil of Australia and the Brazilian Ministry of Health; TRIDENT ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT02699645; Australian New Zealand Clinical Trials Registry number, ACTRN12616000327482.)

pico.P: 脳内出血の既往があり、ベースライン時に収縮期血圧130〜160 mmHgで臨床的に安定した状態の患者
pico.I: 低用量の3種類の降圧薬（テルミサルタン20mg、アムロジピン2.5mg、インダパミド1.25mg）を組み合わせた単一の錠剤（トリプルピル） + 標準治療
pico.C: プラセボ + 標準治療
pico.O:
主要評価項目：最初の再発性脳卒中。トリプルピル群38人（4.6%）vs プラセボ群62人（7.4%）（ハザード比0.61；95%信頼区間0.41〜0.92；P=0.02）。
副次評価項目：主要心血管イベントの発生率は、トリプルピル群6.6% vs プラセボ群9.8%（P=0.04）。血圧コントロール（収縮期血圧<130 mmHg）は、トリプルピル群49.9% vs プラセボ群26.4%（オッズ比3.15；P<0.001）。
